query: Temu US direct sales cease date May 2025 de minimis exemption Trump

{"query":"Temu US direct sales cease date May 2025 de minimis exemption Trump","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1994,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2025/08/29/retail-impact-de-minimis-exemption-ends-globally.html","title":"Retail panic: 'De minimis' exemption ends globally","content":"- The “de minimis” exemption, which allowed shipments valued under $800 to enter the country duty-free, came to an end globally on Friday.
The Biden administration particularly focused on how the exemption allowed goods made with forced labor to make it into the country in violation of the Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act.
Take Coach and Kate Spade’s parent company Tapestry: About 13% to 14% of the company’s sales were previously covered under de minimis and will now be subject to a 30% tariff, according to an estimate by equity research firm Barclays.
“It’s like all of a sudden 70% of your salary has been removed overnight.”
The disruption could end up being a boon for the likes of Amazon and Walmart.","date":"29 août 2025"},{"url":"https://edition.cnn.com/2025/08/03/business/trump-suspends-duty-free-shipments-temu-shein","title":"Trump suspends tax exemption for cheap shipments | CNN Business","content":"Trump eliminated the so-called “de minimis exemption,” which had admitted duty-free shipments of goods worth $800 or less into the United States.
This is more bad news for Chinese retailers and their customers because it shuts down the option of re-routing small shipments duty-free through countries like Vietnam, which is facing a tariff rate of 20%.
It said it would send out all American orders via US-based distributors, adding that its “pricing for US consumers remains unchanged.” But some of Temu’s American buyers subsequently complained of higher prices and items were quickly out of stock.
The Trump administration had first slashed the de minimis exemption on China in May, but then cut the tariff on those cheap packages from 120% to 54%.","date":"3 août 2025"},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cwy79j2n7d4o","title":"Temu to stop selling goods from China directly to US customers","content":""All sales in the US are now handled by locally based sellers, with orders fulfilled from within the country.
The European Union has also proposed plans to scrap duty-free exemptions for parcels worth less than €150 (£127.50; $169.35).","date":"2 mai 2025"},{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/zacharyfolk/2025/07/30/trump-suspends-de-minimis-tariff-exemption-for-goods-under-800/","title":"Trump Globally Suspends Key Tariff Exemption That Let Temu And Shein Ship Most Goods Tariff-Free","content":"President Donald Trump signed an executive order ending the “de minimis” trade exemption from tariffs—a loophole that previously allowed companies like Temu and Shein to ship goods to the United States without paying import duties if they were valued at or under $800.
Packages shipped to the United States valued under $800 will now be subject to “all applicable dues,” the Trump administration said Wednesday.","date":"30 juil. 2025"},{"url":"https://www.chinatalk.nl/how-temu-is-rebounding-from-us-tariffs/","title":"How Temu is rebounding from US tariffs - ChinaTalk","content":"This article is part of the first section of an August 2025 update on the Chinese webshop Temu (Temu Watch #9), published by ChinaTalk’s Ed Sander on Tech Buzz China.
In the first quarter of 2025, Temu’s total merchandise volume increased by 15% to 20% compared with the same period last year.
As we discussed in Temu Watch #8, Temu suspended marketing investment in the US on May 1 when the US eliminated duty-free shipping for small packages under $800.
The tariff on fully managed deliveries has been significantly reduced from the original maximum of 120% to about 54%, which has almost restored the cost of direct shipment to the level before the policy adjustment.
On July 30th, Trump signed a new executive order suspending the duty-free policy for small packages from all countries starting August 29th, completely severing the tax-free access provided by transhipment.
▶️ What the expectations for GMV and profitability are in 2025 and 2026.","date":"16 août 2025"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Temu US direct sales cease date May 2025 de minimis exemption Trump","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.axios.com/2025/05/02/temu-china-trump-tariffs-de-minimis","title":"Temu halts China shipments to US as Trump ends tariff loophole","content":"Temu app next to the Shein and AliExpress apps on a smartphone. Temu changed its website and app to only display listings from products stocked in U.S. warehouses. Temu has stopped shipping products from China to the U.S. ahead of an executive order from President Trump that went into effect on Friday. **Driving the news:** Temu changed its website and app to only display listings from products stocked in U.S. warehouses. * A Temu spokesperson confirmed to Axios that all U.S. sales are now handled by domestic sellers and fulfilled \"from within the country,\" as part of the company's efforts to \"help local merchants reach more customers.\" The company said that pricing for U.S. shoppers \"remains unchanged.\". **The big picture:** Critics of the de minimis exemption say it has hurt U.S. businesses, such as fashion retailer Forever 21, which recently began liquidating its U.S. stores after partly attributing the rise of Shein and Temu to its downfall.","score":0.8124067,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/zacharyfolk/2025/07/30/trump-suspends-de-minimis-tariff-exemption-for-goods-under-800","title":"Trump Globally Suspends Key Tariff Exemption That Let Temu And Shein Ship Most Goods Tariff-Free","content":"# Trump Globally Suspends Key Tariff Exemption That Let Temu And Shein Ship Most Goods Tariff-Free. President Donald Trump signed an executive order ending the “de minimis” trade exemption from tariffs—a loophole that previously allowed companies like Temu and Shein to ship goods to the United States without paying import duties if they were valued at or under $800. Trump signed an executive order ending the exemption on Wednesday. Packages shipped to the United States valued under $800 will now be subject to “all applicable dues,” the Trump administration said Wednesday. In recent years low-cost retailers like Temu and Shein have taken advantage of the de minimis exemption to ship cheap goods directly to American customers without facing levies, but the Trump administration has taken steps to close this loophole—suspending it for goods shipped from China and Hong Kong in April. The executive order is scheduled to go into effect on Aug. 29, the Trump administration said.","score":0.7803451,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2025/08/03/business/trump-suspends-duty-free-shipments-temu-shein","title":"Trump suspends tax exemption for cheap shipments | CNN Business","content":"# Shein’s and Temu’s prices will get hit even harder by the new de minimis rule. This photo taken on June 11, 2024, shows workers producing garments at a textile factory that supplies clothes to fast fashion e-commerce company Shein in Guangzhou in southern China's Guangdong province. This closes a backdoor into the United States for Chinese mega-shippers like Shein and Temu, who could potentially pass the cost of those duties down to consumers. Hours after the de minimis exemption expired for China in early May, Temu announced it was overhauling its shipping model.It said it would send out all American orders via US-based distributors, adding that its “pricing for US consumers remains unchanged.” But some of Temu’s American buyers subsequently complained of higher prices and items were quickly out of stock. The suspension of de minimis will also affect Amazon Haul, a discount competitor to Temu and TikTok Shop that ships directly from China.","score":0.73868835,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/wilmingtoncalifornia/posts/temu-just-made-a-big-changestarting-may-2-2025-theyre-no-longer-shipping-stuff-s/1021195110118973","title":"Temu just made a big change.... - Wilmington Chronicles","content":"## Wilmington Chronicles's Post. ### **Wilmington Chronicles**. Starting May 2, 2025, they’re no longer shipping stuff straight from China to the U.S. Because the U.S. government ended a rule that used to let packages under $800 skip taxes. Now, Temu would have to pay a huge fee—up to 145%—to send stuff from China. Temu is now trying to ship from sellers already in the U.S. instead. Temu’s trying to avoid those extra costs, but shopping on there might feel different now. goods from China directly to US customers","content":"# Temu to stop selling goods from China directly to US customers. Temu has said it will stop selling goods imported from China in the US directly to customers from its platform. The online marketplace said sales would now be handled by \"locally based sellers\", with orders fulfilled from within the country. The move comes as a duty-free rule for low-value packages is closed. Temu, and rival Chinese retail giant Shein, had previously relied on the so-called \"de minimis\" exemption to sell and ship low-value items directly to the US without having to pay duties or import taxes. \"All sales in the US are now handled by locally based sellers, with orders fulfilled from within the country. In the 21st Century, after a series of rises to the threshold, it allowed retailers to ship packages worth less than $800 to US customers without having to pay duties or taxes. Chinese online retailers like Shein and Temu had benefited greatly from the loophole.","score":0.6161283,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.81,"request_id":"81ca48fd-4904-48f2-89c4-44f348e85c63"}
